category_title: Academically gifted students who struggle with an excruciatingly slow reading pace
student_description: Dillan is an academically gifted student who struggles with an excruciatingly slow reading pace and decoding difficulties, leading to frustration and avoidance of independent reading. He uniquely requires alternative text access (like audio), short reading chunks paired with immediate verbal processing, and explicit instruction in leveraging his strong background knowledge to comprehend text.

criteria.rationale: Dillan is academically gifted and thrives when explicitly taught to leverage his strong background knowledge to make text-to-world and text-to-self connections.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes explicit instruction or ongoing practice in using prior knowledge and making connections to derive meaning from text.

criteria.rationale: Dillan relies on social interaction to 'clean his head out' and process complex texts, making peer collaboration essential for his long-term reading engagement.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates regular, structured literature discussions within heterogeneous peer groups.

criteria.rationale: Because Dillan struggles with reading pace and memory load, having a dedicated tool to capture thoughts during reading ensures he is prepared to participate in the peer discussions he values.
criteria.check: The long-term plan introduces a structured format (e.g., a dedicated booklet or graphic organizer) for the student to record questions, connections, and observations while reading.
